title: GitHub Actions の 409 エラー：原因と解決策
reasons: The article accurately defines HTTP 409 Conflict and provides relevant, common causes and solutions specific to GitHub Actions and Azure App Service environments.

Solutions proposed, such as using GitHub Actions' `concurrency` feature, implementing SHA-based retry logic for API file updates, understanding Enterprise-level policies, and checking `mergeable_state` for pull requests, are consistent with official documentation and industry best practices.
Debugging steps leveraging GitHub Actions debug mode, Kudu streaming logs, and Azure CLI commands are practical and correctly described.
Most factual claims are supported by multiple reliable sources, including official documentation (GitHub Docs, Microsoft Learn), GitHub Issues, and technical community discussions.

sources.title: 409 Conflict - HTTP - MDN Web Docs - Mozilla
sources.claim: HTTP status code 409 indicates that the request conflicts with the current state of the resource on the server.

sources.title: RFC 7231 - Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP/1.1): Semantics and Content
sources.claim: HTTP status code 409 indicates that the request conflicts with the current state of the resource on the server.

sources.title: Control the concurrency of workflows and jobs - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: In GitHub Actions, a 409 error often occurs when another deployment is in progress, and the solution is to use the `concurrency` keyword to prevent multiple deployments from running at the same time.

sources.title: 409 Conflict via OneDeploy after concurrency.cancel-in-progress fires · Issue #547 - GitHub
sources.claim: The example error message 'Failed to deploy web package to App Service. Conflict (CODE: 409).' and the phrase 'There is a deployment currently in progress. Please try again when it completes.' are actual error messages seen in Azure App Service deployments via GitHub Actions.

sources.title: 409 Conflict via OneDeploy after concurrency.cancel-in-progress fires · Issue #547 - GitHub
sources.claim: The `scm-deployment-id` is a deployment ID to identify ongoing deployments, often seen in Azure App Service deployment responses.

sources.title: REST API endpoints for repository contents - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: When updating files via GitHub API, a 409 Conflict can occur if the request's SHA (file's current hash value) does not match the actual state on the server, especially with multiple concurrent updates.

sources.title: GitHub API: How to Handle 409 Conflict Errors When Modifying Repository Files
sources.claim: Implementing retry logic by re-fetching the SHA and potentially adding a delay is a common solution to resolve 409 conflicts due to file hash mismatches in the GitHub API.

sources.title: Enforcing policies for GitHub Actions in your enterprise - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: When GitHub Actions policies are set at the Organization or Enterprise level, repository-level permission changes via API (e.g., to `actions/permissions` endpoint) can be restricted, leading to a 409 conflict.

sources.title: REST API endpoints for pull requests - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: When attempting to merge a pull request via the GitHub API, a 409 error is returned if unresolvable merge conflicts exist.

sources.title: Implement PullRequest MergeableState field · Issue #1763 · octokit/octokit.net - GitHub
sources.claim: Checking the `mergeable_state` (e.g., 'dirty' for merge conflicts) of a pull request via the API is a way to detect and address merge conflicts before attempting a merge.

sources.title: Azure Failed to Deploy Web App Service CODE: 409 - Microsoft Learn
sources.claim: When a 409 error occurs using the `azure/webapps-deploy` action, details should be checked in the Azure portal's deployment blade, and Kudu can be used to manage ongoing deployments.

sources.title: az webapp deployment | Microsoft Learn
sources.claim: The Azure CLI command `az webapp deployment list` can be used to check the deployment state of an Azure web app.

sources.title: REST API endpoints for Git trees - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: Using the Git Tree API for bulk updates (creating blobs and trees in a single operation) is more likely to avoid 409 errors when updating multiple files simultaneously compared to individual PUT requests.

sources.title: Enabling debug logging - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: To enable debug mode in GitHub Actions, set the `ACTIONS_STEP_DEBUG` secret to `true` in the workflow.

sources.title: Diagnostic Log Stream · projectkudu/kudu Wiki - GitHub
sources.claim: Kudu streaming logs can be accessed via `https://<your-app-name>.scm.azurewebsites.net/api/logstream` for real-time log tracing.

sources.title: az webapp log | Microsoft Learn
sources.claim: The Azure CLI command `az webapp log tail` starts live log tracing for a web app.

sources.title: Authenticating to the REST API - GitHub Docs
sources.claim: Detailed checks of the GitHub API can be performed using `curl` commands, including for `actions/permissions` endpoints, to diagnose issues.

improvement_suggestions: Optional improvement: add official or primary source URLs for unsupported or weakly sourced claims.

Optional improvement: Add specific URLs to the 'Editor's Note' section for the sources used, as the article states 'Reference URLs are listed in the Editor's Note' but the provided excerpt contains none.

Optional improvement: Consider updating the Azure CLI command example `az webapp deployment list` to `az webapp deploy`, as the former is less direct for troubleshooting and the latter is generally recommended for zip deployments, or add a note about the status of these commands.
Optional improvement: Review current official docs and update changed API/spec/pricing details.
